lead.headline: Rockies walk it off. Angels can't close.
lead.whatHappened: Colorado came from behind to beat Los Angeles 9-8, with the Angels unable to seal a one-run game they had in hand. This is the kind of loss that stings because it was there for the taking.
lead.whyBullet1: The Angels are still searching for consistency in close games, and dropping winnable matchups like this one adds up fast in a tight division race.
lead.whyBullet2: Colorado showed the kind of resilience teams need down the stretch, proving they can steal games on the road against teams that should beat them.
lead.whatToSay: The Angels had one job and couldn't do it. That's on them.

sportsOther: Tigers squeaked past Tampa in a slugfest that nobody asked for.
Brewers demolished the Giants like they owed them money.
Rockies stole one in Anaheim on pure chaos baseball.

golf.headline: Memorial Tournament kicks off at Muirfield Village this week
golf.whyCare1: Muirfield Village is Jack Nicklaus's course and one of the toughest tests on tour—winners here have real credentials.
golf.whyCare2: This is the last major prep event before the US Open in two weeks, so form matters.
golf.watchFor: Who stays patient on the greens. Muirfield Village punishes aggression and rewards guys who lag-putt properly.
golf.whatToSay: Memorial separates the pretenders from guys who can actually compete on brutally hard courses.

f1.headline: Monaco's tight streets demand perfection. Max owns it.
f1.whyCare1: Monaco separates the elite drivers from everyone else because there's zero margin for error on those narrow streets.
f1.whyCare2: Verstappen's won here before and he thrives when the pressure is highest and the track punishes mistakes.
f1.watchFor: First-lap chaos in Turn 1. Monaco's opening corner determines half the race before lap two.
f1.whatToSay: Monaco's the one race where qualifying basically decides everything. You can't pass there, so nail Saturday and you're golden Sunday.
